summary.text: The dominant pattern today is the explosive growth in MCP / Tool Calling and Coding Agent categories, with average daily deltas of 1791 and 3200 stars respectively, far outpacing other categories. This suggests developer focus is shifting toward agentic infrastructure and tool-use capabilities. Meanwhile, RAG and Vector Database projects like stas00/ml-engineering and milvus-io/milvus show sudden breakout acceleration (2.8x and 2.7x), indicating renewed interest in retrieval and embedding pipelines. The cross-source signal from Hacker News discussions about local Qwen and AI's societal impact suggests a parallel conversation about practical vs. hype-driven AI. Forward-looking, the sustained high velocity in agent frameworks may indicate a platform shift toward composable AI systems.

projects.name: awesome-generative-ai-guide
projects.description: A one stop repository for generative AI research updates, interview resources, notebooks and much more!

llmSummary.oneLiner: A comprehensive, community-driven hub for generative AI research, interview prep, free courses, and notebooks.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Centralizes scattered generative AI learning resources (papers, interview questions, free courses, roadmaps) into one curated, regularly updated repository.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 26,915 stars and a sudden acceleration of 2.2 (high), the repo is experiencing a breakout moment. The 24-hour star delta of 23 and 3-day average of 10.7 indicate strong, growing interest. The high acceleration suggests a recent spike in attention, likely driven by the new 'OpenClaw Mastery for Everyone' course announcement or the repository's inclusion in trend lists.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent addition of the 'OpenClaw Mastery for Everyone' course with certification and the repository's feature on Trendshift may have contributed to the sudden breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It aggregates high-demand generative AI learning materials (free courses, interview questions, paper summaries) in one place, appealing to a broad audience of learners and practitioners.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: A valuable bookmark for anyone diving into generative AI — offers structured learning paths, interview prep, and curated paper lists that save hours of searching.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals that the AI developer community craves curated, free, and structured educational content. Repos that reduce friction in learning and job preparation can gain rapid traction.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI/ML students, job seekers preparing for generative AI interviews, developers building LLM applications, and researchers tracking the latest papers.
llmSummary.riskNotes: As an awesome-list, it may become outdated if not actively maintained. The reliance on external links (Notion, Google Forms) could break over time.

projects.name: milvus
projects.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database built for scalable vector ANN search

llmSummary.oneLiner: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database for scalable ANN search, powering AI applications like RAG and image search.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Efficiently storing, indexing, and searching billions of vectors (embeddings) from unstructured data with low latency, high throughput, and horizontal scalability.
llmSummary.whyNow: Milvus has 44,842 stars and a sudden acceleration ratio of 2.4, with 35 stars in the last 24 hours and 107 in the last 7 days. This breakout signals a spike in attention, likely driven by the growing adoption of RAG and LLM-based applications that require vector search at scale.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, recent releases (within 30 days) and continuous commits suggest active development, and the README highlights new features like full-text search with BM25 and hybrid search, which may attract users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Milvus is the leading open-source vector database, essential for RAG pipelines and AI search. Its cloud-native architecture, GPU acceleration, and support for multiple index types make it a go-to choice for developers building scalable AI applications.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Milvus is production-ready for vector search at scale, with a rich ecosystem (SDKs, integrations, managed cloud). Developers should evaluate it for RAG, semantic search, and recommendation systems.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a maturing infrastructure layer for AI: vector databases are becoming as critical as traditional databases. Milvus's sudden breakout reflects the market's shift toward embedding-based retrieval and the need for scalable, real-time vector search.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI engineers, ML teams, and data scientists building RAG systems, semantic search, image/video similarity, or recommendation engines that require high-performance vector search.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Competition from other vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant) and managed services (Zilliz Cloud) may fragment adoption. Complexity of self-hosting a distributed system could deter small teams.

projects.name: cognee
projects.description: Cognee is the open-source AI memory platform for agents. Give your AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions with a self-hosted knowledge graph engine.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Cognee is an open-source AI memory platform that gives agents persistent long-term memory via a self-hosted knowledge graph.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack persistent, structured memory across sessions, making them forget context and repeat mistakes. Cognee solves this by ingesting data in any format, building a knowledge graph with vector and graph reasoning, and providing recall, forget, and improve APIs.
llmSummary.whyNow: Cognee is experiencing a sudden breakout with 43 stars in the last 24 hours, a 7-day average of 23.3 stars/day, and a high acceleration of 2.2. Total stars have surged to 17,904, indicating a rapid spike in attention. The project has a recent release and commits within 7 days, suggesting active development driving momentum.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) likely contributed to the breakout. The README also highlights integrations with Claude Code and a research paper on arXiv, which may have amplified visibility.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Cognee addresses a critical gap in the AI agent ecosystem: persistent memory. With the rise of agentic workflows, developers need a reliable, open-source memory layer. The project's combination of knowledge graphs, vector search, and easy API (remember/recall/forget) makes it practical. The sudden breakout suggests a viral moment, possibly from community adoption or a high-profile mention.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Cognee offers a ready-to-use memory backend for AI agents, reducing the need to build custom graph/vector infrastructure. Its Python SDK and CLI make integration straightforward. Developers building multi-session agents or RAG pipelines should evaluate Cognee as a drop-in memory layer.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward persistent, structured memory as a core component of AI agent architectures. Open-source solutions like Cognee lower the barrier for small teams to add sophisticated memory without proprietary services. The knowledge graph approach may become a standard pattern for context management.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers, RAG pipeline builders, teams building customer support bots, SQL copilots, or any application requiring long-term context across sessions. Also suitable for researchers exploring cognitive architectures.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new and may have scalability or production-readiness gaps. Dependency on external LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI) could be a lock-in risk. The sudden breakout may attract issues and maintenance burden. Self-hosting a knowledge graph adds operational complexity.

projects.name: headroom
projects.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks by 60–95% before they reach an LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: LLM context windows fill up with verbose tool outputs, logs, and RAG chunks, driving up token costs and limiting effective context. Headroom reduces token usage by 60–95% without degrading answer quality, and works as a library, proxy, MCP server, or agent wrapper.
llmSummary.whyNow: Headroom exploded from ~14.6k stars to 37k+ in just 7 days (7d delta: 22,412 stars, 24h delta: 5,410). The acceleration ratio of 4.2 indicates a sudden breakout, likely driven by a viral post or major release. With 37k stars and 2.5k forks, it's one of the fastest-growing AI infrastructure projects on GitHub right now.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and high commit activity suggest a major feature drop or performance benchmark that caught the community's attention. The README mentions output token reduction and Copilot CLI subscription mode, which may have driven adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for AI developers: token costs. It offers immediate, measurable savings (60–95%) with zero code changes via proxy mode, and works with every major agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot). The combination of practical utility, easy onboarding, and strong benchmark results has fueled rapid adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use LLM agents or build AI applications, Headroom can cut your token costs by 60–95% with minimal integration effort. It's production-ready with Python and TypeScript libraries, a proxy, MCP server, and agent wrappers. The reversible compression (CCR) ensures no information loss.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Token compression is becoming a critical infrastructure layer for AI agents. Headroom's approach — content-aware compression, KV-cache alignment, and output token reduction — signals a shift from 'more context' to 'smarter context.' Developers should evaluate compression tools as a standard part of their LLM stack.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI engineers, agent developers, RAG pipeline builders, and anyone using LLMs at scale who wants to reduce token costs without sacrificing answer quality.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very new (sudden breakout) and may have undiscovered edge cases or bugs. The compression algorithms may not preserve accuracy for all content types (though benchmarks look strong). Dependency on local ML models (Kompress-v2-base) adds complexity. The 319 open issues suggest active development but also potential instability.

projects.name: Agent-Reach
projects.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents zero-config access to read and search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more — all free, no API fees.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack native ability to read web content from platforms that require authentication, paid APIs, or complex scraping setups. Agent Reach solves this by providing a unified installer and channel router that selects the best free backend for each platform, handles authentication, and lets agents access content via simple commands.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo exploded from 31,787 stars with a 24h delta of +2,809 and a 7-day delta of +7,284. The acceleration ratio is 3.3 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is a massive spike in attention, likely driven by viral sharing in AI developer communities.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README is comprehensive and was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-16). The project has a recent release and active commits within 7 days. The breakout may be fueled by word-of-mouth among Claude Code and Cursor users who need free web access for their agents.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: getting content from walled-garden platforms without paying for APIs or writing custom scrapers. The 'one CLI, zero API fees' pitch is compelling, and the project's design (multi-backend routing, self-diagnosis, easy install) reduces friction. The Chinese-language README also taps into a large developer base in China.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For AI agent builders, this is a ready-made capability layer that can be integrated into any agent that runs shell commands. It eliminates the need to build and maintain scrapers for multiple platforms. Developers can use it to give their agents web-reading abilities in minutes.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'capability layers' that abstract away the complexity of platform-specific access. Instead of each agent reinventing the wheel for web scraping, a shared open-source layer can handle authentication, backend selection, and fallback routing. This pattern may become standard for agent infrastructure.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers and power users who use Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf, or similar tools and need their agents to read content from Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and other platforms without paying for APIs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party tools and browser cookies for some platforms (Twitter, Reddit, Xiaohongshu), which carries account ban risk if detected. Some backends may break when platforms update their anti-scraping measures. The project's long-term maintenance depends on the author's continued engagement.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks by 60–95% before they reach an LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: LLM context windows fill up with verbose tool outputs, logs, and RAG chunks, driving up token costs and limiting effective context. Headroom reduces token usage by 60–95% without degrading answer quality, and works as a library, proxy, MCP server, or agent wrapper.
llmSummary.whyNow: Headroom exploded from ~14.6k stars to 37k+ in just 7 days (7d delta: 22,412 stars, 24h delta: 5,410). The acceleration ratio of 4.2 indicates a sudden breakout, likely driven by a viral post or major release. With 37k stars and 2.5k forks, it's one of the fastest-growing AI infrastructure projects on GitHub right now.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and high commit activity suggest a major feature drop or performance benchmark that caught the community's attention. The README mentions output token reduction and Copilot CLI subscription mode, which may have driven adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for AI developers: token costs. It offers immediate, measurable savings (60–95%) with zero code changes via proxy mode, and works with every major agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Aider, Copilot). The combination of practical utility, easy onboarding, and strong benchmark results has fueled rapid adoption.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use LLM agents or build AI applications, Headroom can cut your token costs by 60–95% with minimal integration effort. It's production-ready with Python and TypeScript libraries, a proxy, MCP server, and agent wrappers. The reversible compression (CCR) ensures no information loss.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Token compression is becoming a critical infrastructure layer for AI agents. Headroom's approach — content-aware compression, KV-cache alignment, and output token reduction — signals a shift from 'more context' to 'smarter context.' Developers should evaluate compression tools as a standard part of their LLM stack.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI engineers, agent developers, RAG pipeline builders, and anyone using LLMs at scale who wants to reduce token costs without sacrificing answer quality.

modelDemoSignals.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
modelDemoSignals.description: LLM reasoning transparency is a critical affordance for understanding model decisions, mitigating misuse and misalignment, and debugging surprising model behaviors. However, DiffusionGemma performs a larger fraction of its computation in a continuous latent space; does this make its reasoning less transparent? We study this question by decomposing transparency into two components: variable transparency, whether we understand intermediate snapshots of a model's computational state; and algorithmi
modelDemoSignals.summary: LLM reasoning transparency is a critical affordance for understanding model decisions, mitigating misuse and misalignment, and debugging surprising model behaviors. However, DiffusionGemma performs a larger fraction of its computation in a continuous latent space; does this make its reasoning less transparent? We study this question by decomposing transparency into two components: variable transparency, whether we understand intermediate snapshots of a model's computational state; and algorithmic transparency, whether we can use these snapshots to reconstruct the process by which the model arrived at its outputs. Naively, DiffusionGemma has poor variable transparency: its opaque serial depth, the amount of serial computation that occurs in between interpretable model states, seems at first 28.6X higher than the corresponding autoregressive Gemma 4 model. However, we show that we can map the information flowing between denoising steps through an interpretable token bottleneck with no decrease in downstream performance. Treating these intermediate states as interpretable reduces the opaque serial depth to just 1.1X that of Gemma 4. Algorithmic transparency is harder for diffusion models than for autoregressive models because all token predictions in the canvas can change at every denoising step, giving the model the power to implement complicated distributed algorithms during the denoising process. To begin bridging this gap, we conduct a suite of interpretability case studies, uncovering initial evidence of novel diffusion-specific phenomena such as non-chronological reasoning, token and sequence smearing, and intermediate-context reasoning. Finally, we test monitorability, a key application of transparency that measures whether model outputs are useful for downstream tasks. We find that DiffusionGemma is similarly monitorable to Gemma 4.
modelDemoSignals.author: Joshua Engels, Callum McDougall, Bilal Chughtai, Janos Kramar, Senthoran Rajamanoharan, Cindy Wu, Arthur Conmy, Asic Q Chen, Jean Tarbouriech, Min Ma, Brendan O'Donoghue, João Gabriel Lopes de Oliveira, Rohin Shah, Neel Nanda
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, alignment

modelDemoSignals.title: UNIEGO: Proxies as Mediators for Unified Egocentric Video Representation Learning
modelDemoSignals.description: Egocentric video understanding is inherently limited by the narrow perspective of wearable cameras: a single viewpoint, a single modality, a single model cannot capture the full richness of human action. We argue that a truly expressive egocentric representation must subsume complementary knowledge across viewpoints, modalities, and foundation model representations, yet remain deployable from egocentric video alone. To this end, we introduce a hierarchical multi-teacher distillation framework th
modelDemoSignals.summary: Egocentric video understanding is inherently limited by the narrow perspective of wearable cameras: a single viewpoint, a single modality, a single model cannot capture the full richness of human action. We argue that a truly expressive egocentric representation must subsume complementary knowledge across viewpoints, modalities, and foundation model representations, yet remain deployable from egocentric video alone. To this end, we introduce a hierarchical multi-teacher distillation framework that produces UNIEGO, a unified egocentric encoder trained with nine teachers spanning ego-exo viewpoints, RGB, depth, and skeleton modalities, and four foundation models. Rather than distilling directly from heterogeneous teachers whose incompatible architectures and feature geometries induce conflicting gradients, our framework interposes a layer of representation-specific Proxy models that translate diverse teacher knowledge into a homogeneous egocentric space. A second distillation stage, Selective Proxy Distillation (SPD), then adaptively selects, for each training sample, the subset of proxies that are both correct and confident, distilling exclusively from reliable supervision and suppressing erroneous signals. SPD is further stabilized by initializing UNIEGO as a learned convex combination of proxy parameters, placing the unified model in a well-conditioned region of the loss landscape before distillation begins. UNIEGO achieves state-of-the-art performance across three egocentric video understanding tasks - action recognition, video retrieval, and action segmentation on three challenging ego-exo benchmarks, outperforming naive multi-teacher distillation baselines and demonstrating that structured, proxy-mediated knowledge transfer yields richer and more discriminative egocentric representations.
modelDemoSignals.author: Wenhao Chi, Arkaprava Sinha, Dominick Reilly, Hieu Le, Srijan Das

modelDemoSignals.title: Toward Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Under Distribution Shift
modelDemoSignals.description: Calibration aligns a model's predictive uncertainty with the frequencies of its empirical outcomes and is important for understanding and trusting reported probabilities. Recent work shows that enforcing calibration at the level of individual predictors can improve ensemble accuracy and calibration, with mixture-of-experts (MoE) models showing strong empirical improvements in particular; however, the conditions under which calibration helps MoE are not well understood. In this work, we study how
modelDemoSignals.summary: Calibration aligns a model's predictive uncertainty with the frequencies of its empirical outcomes and is important for understanding and trusting reported probabilities. Recent work shows that enforcing calibration at the level of individual predictors can improve ensemble accuracy and calibration, with mixture-of-experts (MoE) models showing strong empirical improvements in particular; however, the conditions under which calibration helps MoE are not well understood. In this work, we study how MoE models behave under distribution shift, focusing on how routing mechanisms interact with expert-level calibration. We show that expert calibration is sufficient to ensure calibration of the overall model under a broad class of distribution shifts in hard-routed models, but is insufficient for calibrating soft-routed models. To address this, we propose an adversarial reweighting that penalizes calibration errors of the routed aggregate under distribution shift, and we demonstrate that it improves the accuracy-calibration tradeoff both on average and on difficult subsets of the data, across model classes, prediction tasks, and distribution shifts.
modelDemoSignals.author: Gina Wong, Drew Prinster, Suchi Saria, Rama Chellappa, Anqi Liu

modelDemoSignals.title: Multi-Task Bayesian In-Context Learning
modelDemoSignals.description: Bayesian predictive inference provides a principled framework for uncertainty quantification, data efficiency, and robust generalization. However, exact inference is often intractable, and scalable approximations may remain computationally expensive or require restrictive modeling assumptions that degrade predictive performance. Prior-Data Fitted and in-context models have recently emerged as an amortized alternative by learning to map datasets directly to predictive distributions, but existing
modelDemoSignals.summary: Bayesian predictive inference provides a principled framework for uncertainty quantification, data efficiency, and robust generalization. However, exact inference is often intractable, and scalable approximations may remain computationally expensive or require restrictive modeling assumptions that degrade predictive performance. Prior-Data Fitted and in-context models have recently emerged as an amortized alternative by learning to map datasets directly to predictive distributions, but existing approaches are tightly coupled to the support of the training prior and lack explicit mechanisms for adapting to new priors at test time, resulting in limited robustness under distribution shift. We introduce a multi-task in-context learning framework for amortized hierarchical Bayesian predictive inference that explicitly represents prior information as a prefix of in-context datasets. A transformer trained on sequences of prior and target tasks learns to adapt its predictions across families of priors. On a suite of evaluations with increasing difficulty, including out-of-meta-distribution priors and priors with high-dimensional latent structures, our method matches oracle Bayesian predictors while being orders of magnitude faster. We further demonstrate its practical relevance on a real-world spatiotemporal temperature prediction benchmark. Code is available at https://github.com/martianmartina/multi-task-bayesian-icl/.
modelDemoSignals.author: Qingyang Zhu, Eric Karl Oermann, Kyunghyun Cho
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
modelDemoSignals.description: Mainstream LLM serving systems reuse prefix work mainly through paged or radix key-value (KV) caches. This is highly effective for high-throughput, high-concurrency serving, but it manages only one positional fragment of execution state: the KV cache. We study the opposite regime: low-latency, small-batch, on-device physical-AI serving, where interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies repeatedly branch, reset, interrupt, and re-enter under tight responsiveness budgets. We introdu
modelDemoSignals.summary: Mainstream LLM serving systems reuse prefix work mainly through paged or radix key-value (KV) caches. This is highly effective for high-throughput, high-concurrency serving, but it manages only one positional fragment of execution state: the KV cache. We study the opposite regime: low-latency, small-batch, on-device physical-AI serving, where interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies repeatedly branch, reset, interrupt, and re-enter under tight responsiveness budgets. We introduce execution-state capsules, a graph-bound checkpoint and restore mechanism for the complete restorable state at a committed boundary. FlashRT is a white-box, backend-facing kernel runtime whose evaluated NVIDIA CUDA backend runs captured graph plans over contiguous static buffers with no block-table indirection. Because the live state is a closed set of named buffers, a capsule can snapshot, restore, fork, or roll back the whole execution boundary, including KV, recurrent state, convolution state, MTP state, and metadata. This moves reuse from token-addressed KV fragments to graph-bound execution-state boundaries. On an RTX 5090, capsule restore is byte-exact at the stored-state level and token-identical under greedy decode. A KV-only ablation diverges, showing that recurrent state is load-bearing. GPU-resident snapshot and restore are sub-millisecond, and TTFT speedup over cold prefill grows from 3.9x at 2k tokens to 27x at 16k tokens. On Jetson AGX Thor and DGX Spark, the same correctness and structural properties hold. Capsules are not a replacement for high-throughput KV-cache serving; they define a complementary latency-first serving point for explicit execution-state reuse.

aihotHighlights.title: 禁止开源AI将是一个错误
aihotHighlights.summary: 近期行政命令、国会提案及对Anthropic最先进模型的境外访问限制，可能开启新一轮AI监管。开源软件已支撑全球90%以上软件并创造8万亿美元经济价值，在教育、创新和竞争三方面持续赋能。Anthropic与OpenAI的封闭模型加剧市场集中，开源（尤其开放权重）是初创公司、教育机构和企业获得替代方案的唯一平衡力量。开源透明性使其更安全，更多工程师可剔除不需要的模型行为或修复漏洞。以中国竞争为由监管开源将适得其反，美国初创公司正依赖包括中国在内的开源模型提升效率。

aihotHighlights.title: Humanize PPT v0.9：为演讲而生的开源PPT Skill
aihotHighlights.summary: Humanize PPT v0.9 是一款专为演讲场景设计的PPT Skill，核心通过AST（Audience， State， Transfer）逻辑重新编排大纲，将页面渲染外包给下游Skill。渲染前先输出4张真实预览页，并将图片、视频素材的占位与生成prompt写入大纲。新增质检环节自动修复常见渲染问题，并支持演讲模式：按S键在独立窗口显示演讲稿备注，按ESC键打开全局索引快速跳页。项目已开源至github.com/LearnPrompt/humanize-ppt，由卡尔 & yc星辰开发。

aihotHighlights.title: 我们在 Elasticsearch 上构建了一个持久化代理内存层，其召回率为0.89
aihotHighlights.summary: Agent Builder 正式上市（GA）。基于 Elasticsearch 的持久化内存层将记忆分为情景、语义、程序三类，分别存入独立索引，各设不同写速率与过期规则。召回采用 BM25 与 Jina v5 稠密向量的 RRF 融合，再经交叉编码器重排序。在 168 道 QA 题评估中，R@10 平均 0.89，零跨租户泄漏。该层可通过支持 MCP 协议的客户端访问，不绑定特定运行时，已开源至 GitHub。

aihotHighlights.title: 阿里开源向量数据库Zvec，UCSD黄碧薇教授提出因果AI第四代范式
aihotHighlights.summary: 阿里开源内部向量数据库Zvec，pip install zvec免费使用，对标Pinecone每月70美元能力。支持十亿向量毫秒级检索，无需单独起服务，全平台兼容；v0.5.0新增原生全文混合搜索。UCSD黄碧薇教授（causal-learn作者）提出AI四代范式：相关性小模型→因果小模型→相关性大模型（LLM）→因果大模型，认为当前正站在第四代门口。其创立的Aether AI完成首轮融资，致力于从视频中自动抽取物理规律，探索下一代因果AI范式。

aihotHighlights.title: DeepSeek研究员开源AutoResearch：AI自主跑通285B模型RL研究闭环
aihotHighlights.summary: DeepSeek研究员Deli Chen将AutoResearch协议开源，并发布Self-play综述论文。其AI智能体首次完全自主地在DeepSeek 285B模型上完成完整RL研究闭环--从实验设计、写代码、提交GPU任务、debug到结论总结，全程零人工干预。系统调用了GRPO工具，被视为持续学习研究的开端。

aihotHighlights.title: Salesforce CodeGen教程：生成、验证并重排序Python函数（含单元测试与安全检查）
aihotHighlights.summary: 本教程实现一个基于Salesforce CodeGen的端到端代码生成工作流。从HuggingFace加载CodeGen模型（支持350M、2B、codegen2-1B、codegen25-7b等版本），通过自然语言提示生成Python函数，随后进行函数提取、语法检查、静态安全检查、单元测试验证、best-of-N候选重排序、多步程序合成、提示词实验、基准可视化及导出。展示了CodeGen作为结构化代码生成流水线的能力，不仅完成代码补全，还能评估、筛选和组织生成结果。

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Code 现已支持 artifacts
aihotHighlights.summary: 从今日起，Claude Code 可将工作进度生成为 artifacts--实时、可分享的交互式网页，涵盖 PR 走查、系统说明、仪表盘、发布清单等。artifacts 基于会话完整上下文（代码库、连接器、对话）自动构建，更新时页面原地刷新，同事即时可见。默认仅作者可见，可分享给组织内成员，由管理员通过组织层级开关和角色权限管控。内部测试中最常见用例为调试：工程师调查事件，Claude Code 分析日志并发布包含时间线、嫌疑提交和错误率图表的 artifact，团队无需再"走过场式汇报"。

aihotHighlights.title: MosaicLeaks： 你的研究智能体能保守秘密吗？
aihotHighlights.summary: 深度研究智能体在结合私有本地文档与外部网页检索时存在隐私泄露风险。MosaicLeaks 提出包含 1，001 条多跳研究链的新任务，每条链交错混合本地与公共子问题。测试发现智能体频繁泄露私有信息，单纯优化任务性能反而加剧泄露。基于此，研究提出隐私感知深度研究（PA-DR）强化学习训练方法，将严格链成功率从 48.7% 提升至 58.7%，同时将答案/全面信息泄露率从 34.0% 降至 9.9%。

aihotHighlights.title: OpenAI 强化学习实现广泛且持久的有益模型
aihotHighlights.summary: OpenAI 通过强化学习在真实对话场景中训练模型，使其展现诚实、认知谦逊、元认知透明、可纠正性、普遍公平性和对人类福祉的关心等有益特质。训练数据涵盖健康、教育、科学、法律、工程等多个领域。训练后模型在数十项独立对齐评测（包括奖励黑客、欺骗、有害建议、规范遵从等）上均表现提升，且这种改善泛化到未参与训练的领域、任务和评分设定。在对抗性提示或微调下，模型仍难以被导向有害行为，表明有益特质强化学习可产生广泛且持久的对齐泛化。

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Enterprise 推出企业托管 MCP 连接器授权管理
aihotHighlights.summary: Claude Enterprise 推出企业托管授权功能，管理员可通过身份提供商（率先支持 Okta）为整个组织配置 MCP 连接器。用户首次登录 Claude 时自动获得授权，无需手动操作，实现零接触设置。该功能基于 Model Context Protocol 的 Enterprise-Managed Authorization 扩展构建，支持 Asana、Atlassian、Canva、Figma、Granola、Linear、Supabase 等 MCP 提供商，Slack 即将支持。授权管理集成到现有 IdP 工作流中，可按组限定范围、通过 IdP 快速撤销授权，并支持要求连接器仅通过 IdP 连接以隔离工作与个人使用。

items.title: 禁止开源AI将是一个错误
items.summary: 近期行政命令、国会提案及对Anthropic最先进模型的境外访问限制，可能开启新一轮AI监管。开源软件已支撑全球90%以上软件并创造8万亿美元经济价值，在教育、创新和竞争三方面持续赋能。Anthropic与OpenAI的封闭模型加剧市场集中，开源（尤其开放权重）是初创公司、教育机构和企业获得替代方案的唯一平衡力量。开源透明性使其更安全，更多工程师可剔除不需要的模型行为或修复漏洞。以中国竞争为由监管开源将适得其反，美国初创公司正依赖包括中国在内的开源模型提升效率。

items.title: Cloudflare 为 AI 智能体推出临时账户
items.summary: Cloudflare 在 Workers 上推出临时账户（Temporary Accounts），允许 AI 智能体直接运行 `wrangler deploy --temporary`，在数秒内获取一个可用的实时 Worker，无需绕开面向人类设计的部署流程。该功能旨在降低智能体部署门槛。

items.title: 禁止开源AI将是一个错误
items.description: 近期行政命令、国会提案及对Anthropic最先进模型的境外访问限制，可能开启新一轮AI监管。开源软件已支撑全球90%以上软件并创造8万亿美元经济价值，在教育、创新和竞争三方面持续赋能。Anthropic与OpenAI的封闭模型加剧市场集中，开源（尤其开放权重）是初创公司、教育机构和企业获得替代方案的唯一平衡力量。开源透明性使其更安全，更多工程师可剔除不需要的模型行为或修复漏洞。以中国竞争为由监管开源将适得其反，美国初创公司正依赖包括中国在内的开源模型提升效率。

items.title: Cloudflare 为 AI 智能体推出临时账户
items.description: Cloudflare 在 Workers 上推出临时账户（Temporary Accounts），允许 AI 智能体直接运行 `wrangler deploy --temporary`，在数秒内获取一个可用的实时 Worker，无需绕开面向人类设计的部署流程。该功能旨在降低智能体部署门槛。

items.title: Humanize PPT v0.9：为演讲而生的开源PPT Skill
items.description: Humanize PPT v0.9 是一款专为演讲场景设计的PPT Skill，核心通过AST（Audience， State， Transfer）逻辑重新编排大纲，将页面渲染外包给下游Skill。渲染前先输出4张真实预览页，并将图片、视频素材的占位与生成prompt写入大纲。新增质检环节自动修复常见渲染问题，并支持演讲模式：按S键在独立窗口显示演讲稿备注，按ESC键打开全局索引快速跳页。项目已开源至github.com/LearnPrompt/humanize-ppt，由卡尔 & yc星辰开发。

items.title: 我们在 Elasticsearch 上构建了一个持久化代理内存层，其召回率为0.89
items.description: Agent Builder 正式上市（GA）。基于 Elasticsearch 的持久化内存层将记忆分为情景、语义、程序三类，分别存入独立索引，各设不同写速率与过期规则。召回采用 BM25 与 Jina v5 稠密向量的 RRF 融合，再经交叉编码器重排序。在 168 道 QA 题评估中，R@10 平均 0.89，零跨租户泄漏。该层可通过支持 MCP 协议的客户端访问，不绑定特定运行时，已开源至 GitHub。

items.title: 阿里开源向量数据库Zvec，UCSD黄碧薇教授提出因果AI第四代范式
items.description: 阿里开源内部向量数据库Zvec，pip install zvec免费使用，对标Pinecone每月70美元能力。支持十亿向量毫秒级检索，无需单独起服务，全平台兼容；v0.5.0新增原生全文混合搜索。UCSD黄碧薇教授（causal-learn作者）提出AI四代范式：相关性小模型→因果小模型→相关性大模型（LLM）→因果大模型，认为当前正站在第四代门口。其创立的Aether AI完成首轮融资，致力于从视频中自动抽取物理规律，探索下一代因果AI范式。

items.title: DeepSeek研究员开源AutoResearch：AI自主跑通285B模型RL研究闭环
items.description: DeepSeek研究员Deli Chen将AutoResearch协议开源，并发布Self-play综述论文。其AI智能体首次完全自主地在DeepSeek 285B模型上完成完整RL研究闭环--从实验设计、写代码、提交GPU任务、debug到结论总结，全程零人工干预。系统调用了GRPO工具，被视为持续学习研究的开端。

items.title: Salesforce CodeGen教程：生成、验证并重排序Python函数（含单元测试与安全检查）
items.description: 本教程实现一个基于Salesforce CodeGen的端到端代码生成工作流。从HuggingFace加载CodeGen模型（支持350M、2B、codegen2-1B、codegen25-7b等版本），通过自然语言提示生成Python函数，随后进行函数提取、语法检查、静态安全检查、单元测试验证、best-of-N候选重排序、多步程序合成、提示词实验、基准可视化及导出。展示了CodeGen作为结构化代码生成流水线的能力，不仅完成代码补全，还能评估、筛选和组织生成结果。

trendEntities.title: chopratejas/headroom

items.title: chopratejas/headroom
items.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

sourceItems.author: chopratejas

trendEntities.summary: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before sending them to an LLM, reducing token usage by 60-95% while preserving answer quality. It offers a library, proxy, and MCP server.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained massive attention on GitHub (37k+ stars, 5.4k in 24h, 22.4k in 7d) as developers seek cost-efficient ways to use LLMs in production, especially for agentic workflows and RAG pipelines.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building LLM-powered applications, especially those using coding agents, RAG systems, or handling large context windows; platform engineers optimizing LLM costs; and teams deploying MCP servers.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Reduces LLM API costs significantly (60-95% fewer tokens) without sacrificing output quality, enabling more scalable and cost-effective AI features in products.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Easy integration via library or proxy; can be dropped into existing agent or RAG pipelines to cut token usage immediately. MCP server support adds flexibility for agent frameworks.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Compression may lose nuance in edge cases; production reliability not yet proven at scale; rapid star growth may be hype-driven; no independent benchmarks on answer quality preservation.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Agent-Reach is an open-source CLI tool that enables AI agents to read and search multiple social platforms (Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu) without API fees.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained significant star momentum (8,412 stars in 7 days, 1,598 in 24h) indicating strong developer interest in multi-platform agentic data access tools.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI agent developers, tool builders, and developers building agents that need real-time social/web data without per-platform API costs.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Enables cost-effective data access for AI agents, potentially reducing integration overhead for startups building agent-based products.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a ready-to-use CLI for agent developers to quickly add web/social reading capabilities, avoiding individual API integrations.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Stars may reflect launch attention; long-term maintenance and reliability of scraping-based approach are unproven. Legal/ToS risks of scraping social platforms not addressed.

llmSummary.whatItIs: A CLI proxy that intercepts LLM API calls and reduces token usage by 60-90% via caching, compression, and prompt optimization, written in Rust with zero dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: Rapid star growth (2,298 in 7 days) and high heat score (420) indicate strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage, especially as token costs remain a pain point.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers using LLM APIs in CI/CD or daily workflows, engineering managers concerned with API costs, and tooling teams building MCP or agentic systems.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly addresses the growing need to control LLM operational costs without sacrificing functionality, relevant for any organization scaling LLM usage.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Offers a drop-in solution to reduce token spend with minimal integration effort; potential to become a standard part of LLM toolchains.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Star growth may reflect novelty and marketing rather than production validation; token reduction claims need independent verification; long-term maintenance and compatibility with evolving LLM APIs are uncertain.

items.title: 禁止开源AI将是一个错误
items.summary: 近期行政命令、国会提案及对Anthropic最先进模型的境外访问限制，可能开启新一轮AI监管。开源软件已支撑全球90%以上软件并创造8万亿美元经济价值，在教育、创新和竞争三方面持续赋能。Anthropic与OpenAI的封闭模型加剧市场集中，开源（尤其开放权重）是初创公司、教育机构和企业获得替代方案的唯一平衡力量。开源透明性使其更安全，更多工程师可剔除不需要的模型行为或修复漏洞。以中国竞争为由监管开源将适得其反，美国初创公司正依赖包括中国在内的开源模型提升效率。

trendEntities.title: Humanize PPT v0.9：为演讲而生的开源PPT Skill
normalizedKeys: title:humanize ppt v0 9 为演讲而生的开源ppt skill

items.title: Humanize PPT v0.9：为演讲而生的开源PPT Skill
items.summary: Humanize PPT v0.9 是一款专为演讲场景设计的PPT Skill，核心通过AST（Audience， State， Transfer）逻辑重新编排大纲，将页面渲染外包给下游Skill。渲染前先输出4张真实预览页，并将图片、视频素材的占位与生成prompt写入大纲。新增质检环节自动修复常见渲染问题，并支持演讲模式：按S键在独立窗口显示演讲稿备注，按ESC键打开全局索引快速跳页。项目已开源至github.com/LearnPrompt/humanize-ppt，由卡尔 & yc星辰开发。

trendEntities.title: 我们在 Elasticsearch 上构建了一个持久化代理内存层，其召回率为0.89
normalizedKeys: title:我们在 elasticsearch 上构建了一个持久化代理内存层 其召回率为0 89

items.title: 我们在 Elasticsearch 上构建了一个持久化代理内存层，其召回率为0.89
items.summary: Agent Builder 正式上市（GA）。基于 Elasticsearch 的持久化内存层将记忆分为情景、语义、程序三类，分别存入独立索引，各设不同写速率与过期规则。召回采用 BM25 与 Jina v5 稠密向量的 RRF 融合，再经交叉编码器重排序。在 168 道 QA 题评估中，R@10 平均 0.89，零跨租户泄漏。该层可通过支持 MCP 协议的客户端访问，不绑定特定运行时，已开源至 GitHub。

trendEntities.title: 阿里开源向量数据库Zvec，UCSD黄碧薇教授提出因果AI第四代范式
normalizedKeys: title:阿里开源向量数据库zvec ucsd黄碧薇教授提出因果ai第四代范式

items.title: 阿里开源向量数据库Zvec，UCSD黄碧薇教授提出因果AI第四代范式
items.summary: 阿里开源内部向量数据库Zvec，pip install zvec免费使用，对标Pinecone每月70美元能力。支持十亿向量毫秒级检索，无需单独起服务，全平台兼容；v0.5.0新增原生全文混合搜索。UCSD黄碧薇教授（causal-learn作者）提出AI四代范式：相关性小模型→因果小模型→相关性大模型（LLM）→因果大模型，认为当前正站在第四代门口。其创立的Aether AI完成首轮融资，致力于从视频中自动抽取物理规律，探索下一代因果AI范式。

trendEntities.title: DeepSeek研究员开源AutoResearch：AI自主跑通285B模型RL研究闭环
normalizedKeys: title:deepseek研究员开源autoresearch ai自主跑通285b模型rl研究闭环

items.title: DeepSeek研究员开源AutoResearch：AI自主跑通285B模型RL研究闭环
items.summary: DeepSeek研究员Deli Chen将AutoResearch协议开源，并发布Self-play综述论文。其AI智能体首次完全自主地在DeepSeek 285B模型上完成完整RL研究闭环--从实验设计、写代码、提交GPU任务、debug到结论总结，全程零人工干预。系统调用了GRPO工具，被视为持续学习研究的开端。

normalizedKeys: title:salesforce codegen教程 生成 验证并重排序python函数 含单元测试与安全检查

items.title: Salesforce CodeGen教程：生成、验证并重排序Python函数（含单元测试与安全检查）
items.summary: 本教程实现一个基于Salesforce CodeGen的端到端代码生成工作流。从HuggingFace加载CodeGen模型（支持350M、2B、codegen2-1B、codegen25-7b等版本），通过自然语言提示生成Python函数，随后进行函数提取、语法检查、静态安全检查、单元测试验证、best-of-N候选重排序、多步程序合成、提示词实验、基准可视化及导出。展示了CodeGen作为结构化代码生成流水线的能力，不仅完成代码补全，还能评估、筛选和组织生成结果。

trendEntities.title: 八部门联合发文力推"人工智能 + 消费"，扩大 AI 手机电脑及智能网联汽车消费
normalizedKeys: title:八部门联合发文力推 人工智能 消费 扩大 ai 手机电脑及智能网联汽车消费

items.title: 八部门联合发文力推"人工智能 + 消费"，扩大 AI 手机电脑及智能网联汽车消费
items.summary: 商务部等8部门近日印发《关于加快"人工智能 + 消费"发展的实施意见》，从5方面提出17条举措。重点包括：扩大AI手机电脑、智能家居、智能网联汽车、智能穿戴、AI机器人等供给；推动AI与居家、养老、文旅、餐饮、教育等融合；促进AI在批发零售、电商、物流领域应用；建设集聚区和体验中心，开展产品租赁、共享、试用等模式创新；完善基础设施、标准体系和监管，推动互联互通。商务部将协同落实，促进AI进千家万户。

trendEntities.title: AI 员工 Viktor 登陆 Microsoft Teams，年化收入达 2000 万美元
normalizedKeys: title:ai 员工 viktor 登陆 microsoft teams 年化收入达 2000 万美元

items.title: AI 员工 Viktor 登陆 Microsoft Teams，年化收入达 2000 万美元
items.summary: AI 员工 Viktor 在 Slack 上实现 2000 万美元年化收入（无销售团队、未大规模推广），现已正式进驻 Microsoft Teams。Viktor 定位为零门槛 AI：用户无需学习、无需提示词，像 @同事 一样提及即可获得完整工作成果，甚至无需主动 @ 也能自动完成。产品面向 Teams 的 3.2 亿用户，助力企业内部运营和管理人员零学习成本使用 AI。即日起免费试用，含 100 美元信用额度，无需绑定信用卡。

items.summary: OpenClaw 已内置 OpenRouter 支持，一条命令即可为 AI 智能体配置统一密钥、统一账单，并实现跨 300 多个模型的自动故障转移。同时提供具体设置步骤以及常见错误的修复方法。

trendEntities.title: Claude Code 现已支持 artifacts
normalizedKeys: title:claude code 现已支持 artifacts

items.title: Claude Code 现已支持 artifacts
items.summary: 从今日起，Claude Code 可将工作进度生成为 artifacts--实时、可分享的交互式网页，涵盖 PR 走查、系统说明、仪表盘、发布清单等。artifacts 基于会话完整上下文（代码库、连接器、对话）自动构建，更新时页面原地刷新，同事即时可见。默认仅作者可见，可分享给组织内成员，由管理员通过组织层级开关和角色权限管控。内部测试中最常见用例为调试：工程师调查事件，Claude Code 分析日志并发布包含时间线、嫌疑提交和错误率图表的 artifact，团队无需再"走过场式汇报"。

trendEntities.title: MosaicLeaks： 你的研究智能体能保守秘密吗？
normalizedKeys: title:mosaicleaks 你的研究智能体能保守秘密吗

items.title: MosaicLeaks： 你的研究智能体能保守秘密吗？
items.summary: 深度研究智能体在结合私有本地文档与外部网页检索时存在隐私泄露风险。MosaicLeaks 提出包含 1，001 条多跳研究链的新任务，每条链交错混合本地与公共子问题。测试发现智能体频繁泄露私有信息，单纯优化任务性能反而加剧泄露。基于此，研究提出隐私感知深度研究（PA-DR）强化学习训练方法，将严格链成功率从 48.7% 提升至 58.7%，同时将答案/全面信息泄露率从 34.0% 降至 9.9%。

trendEntities.title: OpenAI 强化学习实现广泛且持久的有益模型
normalizedKeys: title:openai 强化学习实现广泛且持久的有益模型

items.title: OpenAI 强化学习实现广泛且持久的有益模型
items.summary: OpenAI 通过强化学习在真实对话场景中训练模型，使其展现诚实、认知谦逊、元认知透明、可纠正性、普遍公平性和对人类福祉的关心等有益特质。训练数据涵盖健康、教育、科学、法律、工程等多个领域。训练后模型在数十项独立对齐评测（包括奖励黑客、欺骗、有害建议、规范遵从等）上均表现提升，且这种改善泛化到未参与训练的领域、任务和评分设定。在对抗性提示或微调下，模型仍难以被导向有害行为，表明有益特质强化学习可产生广泛且持久的对齐泛化。

items.title: Claude Enterprise 推出企业托管 MCP 连接器授权管理
items.summary: Claude Enterprise 推出企业托管授权功能，管理员可通过身份提供商（率先支持 Okta）为整个组织配置 MCP 连接器。用户首次登录 Claude 时自动获得授权，无需手动操作，实现零接触设置。该功能基于 Model Context Protocol 的 Enterprise-Managed Authorization 扩展构建，支持 Asana、Atlassian、Canva、Figma、Granola、Linear、Supabase 等 MCP 提供商，Slack 即将支持。授权管理集成到现有 IdP 工作流中，可按组限定范围、通过 IdP 快速撤销授权，并支持要求连接器仅通过 IdP 连接以隔离工作与个人使用。

trendEntities.title: 驾驭 Claude Code：CLAUDE.md、技能、钩子、规则、子智能体等
normalizedKeys: title:驾驭 claude code claude md 技能 钩子 规则 子智能体等

items.title: 驾驭 Claude Code：CLAUDE.md、技能、钩子、规则、子智能体等
items.summary: Claude Code 提供七种自定义指令方式：CLAUDE.md（根目录始终加载，子目录按需加载）、规则（无范围或路径范围）、技能（按需调用，共享 token 预算）、子智能体（隔离上下文运行并返回最终消息）、钩子（生命周期事件触发，绕过压缩）、输出样式（注入系统提示，永不压缩）和附加系统提示（CLI 标志，仅单次有效）。每种方式在加载时机、压缩行为、上下文成本和适用场景上各有不同，例如 CLAUDE.md 适合存放构建命令与编码规范，路径范围规则避免无关上下文消耗，子智能体用于并行隔离任务，钩子用于确定性自动化（如运行 linter 或备份聊天记录）。

trendEntities.title: AI数据中心获政府强制电网快车道
normalizedKeys: title:ai数据中心获政府强制电网快车道

items.title: AI数据中心获政府强制电网快车道
items.summary: 美国联邦能源监管委员会（FERC）命令六大电网运营商为数据中心等大型用户提供快速并网通道，数据中心需承担并网费用。FERC同时要求运营商考虑"替代输电技术"，并在30天内报告剩余发电容量、60天内审查本区域电价。指令并未解决发电容量短缺问题。数据中心电力需求预计到2035年增长近三倍，而电网运营商此前长期应对近乎零的需求增长。据Bloomberg，部分地区批发电价较五年前上涨了267%。

trendEntities.title: 企业版新用量分析与更新的支出控制
normalizedKeys: title:企业版新用量分析与更新的支出控制

items.title: 企业版新用量分析与更新的支出控制
items.summary: OpenAI 为 ChatGPT Enterprise 推出信用额度用量分析与更新的支出控制功能。全局管理控制台（Global Admin Console）统一展示 ChatGPT 和 Codex 的信用消耗，支持按时间、用户、产品、模型追踪用量趋势。管理员可为整个工作区设置默认限额，按群组配置额度，并为个人设置叠加限制。员工可查看个人用量并申请增加额度（附工作上下文）。这些功能即日起可用。

sourceItems.title: 企业版新用量分析与更新的支出控制
sourceItems.summary: OpenAI 为 ChatGPT Enterprise 推出信用额度用量分析与更新的支出控制功能。全局管理控制台（Global Admin Console）统一展示 ChatGPT 和 Codex 的信用消耗，支持按时间、用户、产品、模型追踪用量趋势。管理员可为整个工作区设置默认限额，按群组配置额度，并为个人设置叠加限制。员工可查看个人用量并申请增加额度（附工作上下文）。这些功能即日起可用。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic Project Fetch 第二阶段：Claude Opus 4.7 自主完成任务，速度比人类团队快约20倍
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic project fetch 第二阶段 claude opus 4 7 自主完成任务 速度比人类团队快约20倍

items.title: Anthropic Project Fetch 第二阶段：Claude Opus 4.7 自主完成任务，速度比人类团队快约20倍
items.summary: Anthropic 发布 Project Fetch 实验第二阶段结果。在2024年8月原始实验中，配备 Claude Opus 4.1 的人类团队在操控四足机器人时显著超越无 AI 团队。新实验中，Claude Opus 4.7 无需人类协助即完成所有任务，速度比最快人类团队快约20倍，比无 Claude 团队快37倍以上，编码量减少近10倍。模型在传感器连接、路径规划等环节表现出色，但在精确移动沙滩球等闭环控制任务上仍存在困难。这些进展源于通用模型规模化，而非针对机器人领域的专项优化。

trendEntities.title: 我国首部L3/L4自动驾驶强制性国标公示：2027年7月起实施
normalizedKeys: title:我国首部l3 l4自动驾驶强制性国标公示 2027年7月起实施

items.title: 我国首部L3/L4自动驾驶强制性国标公示：2027年7月起实施
items.summary: 工信部6月16日就《智能网联汽车自动驾驶系统安全要求》等2项强制性国标公开征求意见，公示至6月24日，建议2027年7月1日起实施。该标准系我国首部针对L3/L4的强制性国标，要求系统安全水平至少达到"合格且专注驾驶人"，引入Safety Case机制。L3重点规范人机交接，L4强调自身风险处置、不得依赖远程协助。新申请车型实施日起执行，已获批车型有约一年过渡期。

trendEntities.title: Nature两篇研究：MIRA和AMIE诊断与治疗计划媲美甚至超越医生
normalizedKeys: title:nature两篇研究 mira和amie诊断与治疗计划媲美甚至超越医生

items.title: Nature两篇研究：MIRA和AMIE诊断与治疗计划媲美甚至超越医生
items.summary: 德国团队开发的MIRA智能体在模拟电子病历中操作85，000种选项，500余例急诊诊断准确率88.9%，对比测试（311例）得分87.8%，高于资深专科医生（78.1%）和混合团队（71.1%）。MIRA在阑尾炎（98.6%）和胰腺炎（92.3%）最佳，未发现危险药物交互或剂量错误，性能不受语言影响，代码已公开。谷歌AMIE采用双智能体架构，在100个多访视病例中治疗计划适切率95%（初级保健医生72%），并在药物知识基准RxQA上超过医生。两项研究均警告模拟环境与现实存在差距，实际性能可能更低。

raw.titleEn: AI systems rival doctors in new Nature studies， but one result suggests the tech won't age well

sourceItems.title: Nature两篇研究：MIRA和AMIE诊断与治疗计划媲美甚至超越医生
sourceItems.summary: 德国团队开发的MIRA智能体在模拟电子病历中操作85，000种选项，500余例急诊诊断准确率88.9%，对比测试（311例）得分87.8%，高于资深专科医生（78.1%）和混合团队（71.1%）。MIRA在阑尾炎（98.6%）和胰腺炎（92.3%）最佳，未发现危险药物交互或剂量错误，性能不受语言影响，代码已公开。谷歌AMIE采用双智能体架构，在100个多访视病例中治疗计划适切率95%（初级保健医生72%），并在药物知识基准RxQA上超过医生。两项研究均警告模拟环境与现实存在差距，实际性能可能更低。

trendEntities.title: Adobe 为 Photoshop、Premiere 等多款 Creative Cloud 应用加入 AI 智能体
normalizedKeys: title:adobe 为 photoshop premiere 等多款 creative cloud 应用加入 ai 智能体

items.title: Adobe 为 Photoshop、Premiere 等多款 Creative Cloud 应用加入 AI 智能体
items.summary: Adobe 将其"创意智能体"扩展至 Photoshop、Premiere 等应用，以公开测试形式提供 AI Assistant。该智能体可自动完成多步骤常规任务，如 Premiere 分拣素材和粗剪、Photoshop 换背景、Illustrator 批量生成文件、InDesign 更新版式等。Firefly 新增面向个人创作者的品牌套件、产品图转短视频及 Quick Cut 自动剪辑功能。Adobe 工具已集成至 ChatGPT、Claude 及 Microsoft 365 Copilot，Google Gemini 和 Slack 集成即将推出。

trendEntities.title: 皮尤民调：63%美国人认为AI发展太快，ChatGPT使用率翻番
normalizedKeys: title:皮尤民调 63 美国人认为ai发展太快 chatgpt使用率翻番

items.title: 皮尤民调：63%美国人认为AI发展太快，ChatGPT使用率翻番
items.summary: 皮尤研究中心最新民调显示，63%美国人认为AI发展速度过快。ChatGPT使用率较2023年翻番，44%受访者曾使用。49%偶尔使用聊天机器人，仅16%认为AI会有积极社会影响。青年群体使用率更高但更悲观：18-29岁中66%用过，48%认为有负面影响，仅14%看好。30-49岁中34%每天至少使用一次。约四成用AI处理工作，30%认为提高效率，28%认为帮助获取信息。此外，66%成年人担心AI传播错误信息。
items.description: 皮尤研究中心最新民调显示，63%美国人认为AI发展速度过快。ChatGPT使用率较2023年翻番，44%受访者曾使用。49%偶尔使用聊天机器人，仅16%认为AI会有积极社会影响。青年群体使用率更高但更悲观：18-29岁中66%用过，48%认为有负面影响，仅14%看好。30-49岁中34%每天至少使用一次。约四成用AI处理工作，30%认为提高效率，28%认为帮助获取信息。此外，66%成年人担心AI传播错误信息。

trendEntities.title: ChatGPT 图像生成器可被绕过滤镜生成暴力和色情内容
normalizedKeys: title:chatgpt 图像生成器可被绕过滤镜生成暴力和色情内容

items.title: ChatGPT 图像生成器可被绕过滤镜生成暴力和色情内容
items.summary: Mindgard 红队研究发现，ChatGPT 的图像生成器可通过简单提示词轻易绕过内容过滤器，在未直接请求的情况下自动生成性暴力、血腥谋杀等露骨图像。一个热门的"恢复照片"提示词因输入模糊而绕过输入过滤器，结果如同俄罗斯轮盘赌；进一步添加虚假图像 ID 和"不做审查"指令后，模型持续生成高度性化女性图像，甚至出现被捆绑殴打的尸体，并自动赋予惊悚标题。研究指出，OpenAI 此前声称修复的裸体问题仍未解决，暴露了 AI 工具广泛可及性与不足内容过滤的现实风险。

trendEntities.title: GPT-5.5 Instant提升ChatGPT健康智能

items.title: GPT-5.5 Instant提升ChatGPT健康智能
items.summary: 每周超2.3亿用户通过ChatGPT获取健康信息。GPT-5.5 Instant在健康评估中表现显著提升，最具挑战性评测上达到前沿Thinking模型水平，已面向所有免费用户开放。基于医生编写的HealthBench和HealthBench Professional评估，其回复在准确性、安全性和沟通质量上优于医生手写回复及早期模型，故障模式发生率更低。近两个月生产流量显示，健康类回复事实性问题率下降71%。

trendEntities.title: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0 API 服务，开启邀测
normalizedKeys: title:火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3 0 api 服务 开启邀测

items.title: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0 API 服务，开启邀测
items.summary: 火山引擎上线豆包实时语音模型3.0（Seeduplex）API 服务并开启邀测。该模型为原生全双工端到端语音大模型，具备精准遵循、抗干扰、动态判停三大优势。可在多人对话中安静待命，指定话题出现时主动加入；支持通过自定义工具在实时交互中完成预定日历、发送邮件等任务。抗干扰力提升，误回复率与误打断率大幅降低；判停延迟缩短约250ms，复杂场景抢话比例下降40%，用户主动打断延迟缩短约300ms。适用于汽车智能座舱、智能硬件、智能客服等场景。

trendEntities.title: 八部门：用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策，支持消费者购买 AI 相关产品
normalizedKeys: title:八部门 用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策 支持消费者购买 ai 相关产品

items.title: 八部门：用好个人消费贷款财政贴息政策，支持消费者购买 AI 相关产品
items.summary: 商务部等八部门6月18日发布关于加快"人工智能+消费"发展的实施意见。其中提到加大财政资金支持，落实数码和智能产品购新政策，鼓励地方在消费品以旧换新框架内自主制定补贴，重点支持新一代智能终端消费。增加AI手机、智能电脑、智能电视、智能家居、AI眼镜、智能网联汽车、人形机器人等产品供给，培育智能穿戴消费市场。同时加快AI在居家服务、养老服务、文化旅游、住宿餐饮、教育教学等领域的应用，建设AI商品首发平台，举办"人工智能进万家"活动。

items.title: OpenAI与哈佛等合作研究：o3 Deep Research模型辅助诊断儿童罕见病，额外诊断率4.8%
items.summary: 波士顿儿童医院、哈佛大学与OpenAI合作，在《NEJM AI》发表研究。团队使用OpenAI o3 Deep Research推理模型重新分析376例此前未确诊的罕见病案例，产出基于证据的候选解释。经专家评审、额外检测和临床确认，医生在18例中建立诊断，额外诊断率达4.8%。研究显示，AI辅助工作流可帮助专家在未解病例中生成可检验假设，使定期再分析更具可扩展性。模型不直接诊断或做临床决策，仅提供证据链供专家审查。

items.title: DeepSeek 识图模式正式上线 App 和网页端
items.summary: DeepSeek 识图模式于6月18日在网页和 App 端正式上线，与快速模式、专家模式并列。开启后用户可直接上传图片让 DeepSeek 识别图像，能力超越简单文字提取。目前 App 端仍显示"图片理解功能内测中"，网页端无此提示。该模式背后的多模态模型技术细节于今年4月公开，核心框架为"Thinking with Visual Primitives（以视觉原语思考）"。

How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?

items.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
items.description: LLM reasoning transparency is a critical affordance for understanding model decisions, mitigating misuse and misalignment, and debugging surprising model behaviors. However, DiffusionGemma performs a larger fraction of its computation in a continuous latent space; does this make its reasoning less transparent? We study this question by decomposing transparency into two components: variable transparency, whether we understand intermediate snapshots of a model's computational state; and algorithmi
items.summary: LLM reasoning transparency is a critical affordance for understanding model decisions, mitigating misuse and misalignment, and debugging surprising model behaviors. However, DiffusionGemma performs a larger fraction of its computation in a continuous latent space; does this make its reasoning less transparent? We study this question by decomposing transparency into two components: variable transparency, whether we understand intermediate snapshots of a model's computational state; and algorithmic transparency, whether we can use these snapshots to reconstruct the process by which the model arrived at its outputs. Naively, DiffusionGemma has poor variable transparency: its opaque serial depth, the amount of serial computation that occurs in between interpretable model states, seems at first 28.6X higher than the corresponding autoregressive Gemma 4 model. However, we show that we can map the information flowing between denoising steps through an interpretable token bottleneck with no decrease in downstream performance. Treating these intermediate states as interpretable reduces the opaque serial depth to just 1.1X that of Gemma 4. Algorithmic transparency is harder for diffusion models than for autoregressive models because all token predictions in the canvas can change at every denoising step, giving the model the power to implement complicated distributed algorithms during the denoising process. To begin bridging this gap, we conduct a suite of interpretability case studies, uncovering initial evidence of novel diffusion-specific phenomena such as non-chronological reasoning, token and sequence smearing, and intermediate-context reasoning. Finally, we test monitorability, a key application of transparency that measures whether model outputs are useful for downstream tasks. We find that DiffusionGemma is similarly monitorable to Gemma 4.
items.author: Joshua Engels, Callum McDougall, Bilal Chughtai, Janos Kramar, Senthoran Rajamanoharan, Cindy Wu, Arthur Conmy, Asic Q Chen, Jean Tarbouriech, Min Ma, Brendan O'Donoghue, João Gabriel Lopes de Oliveira, Rohin Shah, Neel Nanda

items.title: UNIEGO: Proxies as Mediators for Unified Egocentric Video Representation Learning
items.description: Egocentric video understanding is inherently limited by the narrow perspective of wearable cameras: a single viewpoint, a single modality, a single model cannot capture the full richness of human action. We argue that a truly expressive egocentric representation must subsume complementary knowledge across viewpoints, modalities, and foundation model representations, yet remain deployable from egocentric video alone. To this end, we introduce a hierarchical multi-teacher distillation framework th
items.summary: Egocentric video understanding is inherently limited by the narrow perspective of wearable cameras: a single viewpoint, a single modality, a single model cannot capture the full richness of human action. We argue that a truly expressive egocentric representation must subsume complementary knowledge across viewpoints, modalities, and foundation model representations, yet remain deployable from egocentric video alone. To this end, we introduce a hierarchical multi-teacher distillation framework that produces UNIEGO, a unified egocentric encoder trained with nine teachers spanning ego-exo viewpoints, RGB, depth, and skeleton modalities, and four foundation models. Rather than distilling directly from heterogeneous teachers whose incompatible architectures and feature geometries induce conflicting gradients, our framework interposes a layer of representation-specific Proxy models that translate diverse teacher knowledge into a homogeneous egocentric space. A second distillation stage, Selective Proxy Distillation (SPD), then adaptively selects, for each training sample, the subset of proxies that are both correct and confident, distilling exclusively from reliable supervision and suppressing erroneous signals. SPD is further stabilized by initializing UNIEGO as a learned convex combination of proxy parameters, placing the unified model in a well-conditioned region of the loss landscape before distillation begins. UNIEGO achieves state-of-the-art performance across three egocentric video understanding tasks - action recognition, video retrieval, and action segmentation on three challenging ego-exo benchmarks, outperforming naive multi-teacher distillation baselines and demonstrating that structured, proxy-mediated knowledge transfer yields richer and more discriminative egocentric representations.
items.author: Wenhao Chi, Arkaprava Sinha, Dominick Reilly, Hieu Le, Srijan Das

SPD is further stabilized by initializing UNIEGO as a learned convex combination of proxy parameters, placing the unified model in a well-conditioned region of the loss landscape before distillation begins. UNIEGO achieves state-of-the-art performance across three egocentric video understanding tasks - action recognition, video retrieval, and action segmentation on three challenging ego-exo benchmarks, outperforming naive multi-teacher distillation baselines and demonstrating that structured, proxy-mediated knowledge transfer yields richer and more discriminative egocentric representations.

trendEntities.title: Toward Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Under Distribution Shift
normalizedKeys: title:toward calibrated mixture of experts under distribution shift

items.title: Toward Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Under Distribution Shift
items.description: Calibration aligns a model's predictive uncertainty with the frequencies of its empirical outcomes and is important for understanding and trusting reported probabilities. Recent work shows that enforcing calibration at the level of individual predictors can improve ensemble accuracy and calibration, with mixture-of-experts (MoE) models showing strong empirical improvements in particular; however, the conditions under which calibration helps MoE are not well understood. In this work, we study how
items.summary: Calibration aligns a model's predictive uncertainty with the frequencies of its empirical outcomes and is important for understanding and trusting reported probabilities. Recent work shows that enforcing calibration at the level of individual predictors can improve ensemble accuracy and calibration, with mixture-of-experts (MoE) models showing strong empirical improvements in particular; however, the conditions under which calibration helps MoE are not well understood. In this work, we study how MoE models behave under distribution shift, focusing on how routing mechanisms interact with expert-level calibration. We show that expert calibration is sufficient to ensure calibration of the overall model under a broad class of distribution shifts in hard-routed models, but is insufficient for calibrating soft-routed models. To address this, we propose an adversarial reweighting that penalizes calibration errors of the routed aggregate under distribution shift, and we demonstrate that it improves the accuracy-calibration tradeoff both on average and on difficult subsets of the data, across model classes, prediction tasks, and distribution shifts.
items.author: Gina Wong, Drew Prinster, Suchi Saria, Rama Chellappa, Anqi Liu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag

trendEntities.title: Multi-Task Bayesian In-Context Learning
normalizedKeys: title:multi task bayesian in context learning

items.title: Multi-Task Bayesian In-Context Learning
items.description: Bayesian predictive inference provides a principled framework for uncertainty quantification, data efficiency, and robust generalization. However, exact inference is often intractable, and scalable approximations may remain computationally expensive or require restrictive modeling assumptions that degrade predictive performance. Prior-Data Fitted and in-context models have recently emerged as an amortized alternative by learning to map datasets directly to predictive distributions, but existing
items.summary: Bayesian predictive inference provides a principled framework for uncertainty quantification, data efficiency, and robust generalization. However, exact inference is often intractable, and scalable approximations may remain computationally expensive or require restrictive modeling assumptions that degrade predictive performance. Prior-Data Fitted and in-context models have recently emerged as an amortized alternative by learning to map datasets directly to predictive distributions, but existing approaches are tightly coupled to the support of the training prior and lack explicit mechanisms for adapting to new priors at test time, resulting in limited robustness under distribution shift. We introduce a multi-task in-context learning framework for amortized hierarchical Bayesian predictive inference that explicitly represents prior information as a prefix of in-context datasets. A transformer trained on sequences of prior and target tasks learns to adapt its predictions across families of priors. On a suite of evaluations with increasing difficulty, including out-of-meta-distribution priors and priors with high-dimensional latent structures, our method matches oracle Bayesian predictors while being orders of magnitude faster. We further demonstrate its practical relevance on a real-world spatiotemporal temperature prediction benchmark. Code is available at https://github.com/martianmartina/multi-task-bayesian-icl/.
items.author: Qingyang Zhu, Eric Karl Oermann, Kyunghyun Cho
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: benchmark, evaluation

items.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
items.description: Mainstream LLM serving systems reuse prefix work mainly through paged or radix key-value (KV) caches. This is highly effective for high-throughput, high-concurrency serving, but it manages only one positional fragment of execution state: the KV cache. We study the opposite regime: low-latency, small-batch, on-device physical-AI serving, where interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies repeatedly branch, reset, interrupt, and re-enter under tight responsiveness budgets. items.summary: Mainstream LLM serving systems reuse prefix work mainly through paged or radix key-value (KV) caches. This is highly effective for high-throughput, high-concurrency serving, but it manages only one positional fragment of execution state: the KV cache. We study the opposite regime: low-latency, small-batch, on-device physical-AI serving, where interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies repeatedly branch, reset, interrupt, and re-enter under tight responsiveness budgets. We introduce execution-state capsules, a graph-bound checkpoint and restore mechanism for the complete restorable state at a committed boundary. FlashRT is a white-box, backend-facing kernel runtime whose evaluated NVIDIA CUDA backend runs captured graph plans over contiguous static buffers with no block-table indirection. Because the live state is a closed set of named buffers, a capsule can snapshot, restore, fork, or roll back the whole execution boundary, including KV, recurrent state, convolution state, MTP state, and metadata. This moves reuse from token-addressed KV fragments to graph-bound execution-state boundaries. On an RTX 5090, capsule restore is byte-exact at the stored-state level and token-identical under greedy decode. A KV-only ablation diverges, showing that recurrent state is load-bearing. GPU-resident snapshot and restore are sub-millisecond, and TTFT speedup over cold prefill grows from 3.9x at 2k tokens to 27x at 16k tokens. On Jetson AGX Thor and DGX Spark, the same correctness and structural properties hold. Capsules are not a replacement for high-throughput KV-cache serving; they define a complementary latency-first serving point for explicit execution-state reuse.
items.author: Liang Su
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, rag

trendEntities.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
normalizedKeys: title:ledgeragent structured state for policy adherent tool calling agents

items.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
items.description: Policy-adherent tool-calling agents in customer-service domains must maintain task states across turns while calling tools and obeying domain policies. Task states consist of relevant facts, identifiers, constraints, and conditions observed through user interaction and tool calls. In standard agents, task states are not represented separately. Observations, tool returns, and policy instructions are placed in the prompt, leaving agents to reconstruct the relevant states from the prompt each time
items.summary: Policy-adherent tool-calling agents in customer-service domains must maintain task states across turns while calling tools and obeying domain policies. Task states consist of relevant facts, identifiers, constraints, and conditions observed through user interaction and tool calls. In standard agents, task states are not represented separately. Observations, tool returns, and policy instructions are placed in the prompt, leaving agents to reconstruct the relevant states from the prompt each time they decide what to do next. This design makes state management implicit, creating two common failure modes. An agent may retrieve the right facts but later ground its decision in stale, missing, or incorrect information; and a syntactically valid tool call may still violate a domain policy that depends on the current task state. We introduce \textsc{LedgerAgent}, an inference-time method for tool-calling agents that maintains observed task states in a separate ledger and renders the states into the prompt. The ledger is also used to check state-dependent policy constraints before environment-changing tool calls are executed, blocking policy violations. Across four customer-service domains and a mixed panel of open- and closed-weight models, \textsc{LedgerAgent} improves average pass\textasciicircum{}k over a standard prompt-based tool-calling approach, with the largest gains under stricter multi-trial consistency metrics.
items.author: Md Nayem Uddin, Amir Saeidi, Eduardo Blanco, Chitta Baral
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag

trendEntities.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
normalizedKeys: title:stylisticbias a few human visual cues drive most social biases in mllms

items.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
items.description: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly deployed in personally and societally consequential settings, yet the visual cues that shape how these models judge people remain poorly understood. Prior work often compares different (groups of) individuals, making it difficult to separate appearance effects from identity differences. We introduce StylisticBias, a controlled benchmark for evaluating attribute-level social bias in MLLMs. We generate 500 photorealistic base faces and crea
items.summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly deployed in personally and societally consequential settings, yet the visual cues that shape how these models judge people remain poorly understood. Prior work often compares different (groups of) individuals, making it difficult to separate appearance effects from identity differences. We introduce StylisticBias, a controlled benchmark for evaluating attribute-level social bias in MLLMs. We generate 500 photorealistic base faces and create about 50 single-attribute variations per face, producing about 25K images. This design keeps identity fixed and changes one visual attribute at a time. It lets us measure how specific cues shift model judgments. We evaluate six MLLMs across 25 binary social judgment scenarios. We find that age and body type dominate identity-level effects, while fashion style and other visual cues drive the largest attribute-level shifts. We further find that about 15 attributes account for nearly 80\% of the total variation, showing that bias is concentrated in a small set of visual cues. Sensitivity is strongest in judgments that are semantically aligned with appearance, especially socioeconomic and style-related judgments. We release StylisticBias as a benchmark for fine-grained bias evaluation in multimodal models. Code and dataset: https://github.com/timo-cavelius/StylisticBias and https://hf.co/datasets/shaghayegh/stylistic-bias-dataset.
items.author: Shaghayegh Kolli, Timo Cavelius, Nafiseh Nikeghbal, Samantha Dalal, Jana Diesner
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, benchmark, evaluation

items.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
items.description: Multimodal foundation models have advanced rapidly thanks to large optical benchmarks, but comparable resources for synthetic aperture radar (SAR) remain limited. Existing SAR--optical datasets largely rely on low-resolution, intensity-only Ground Range Detected~(GRD) products and do not preserve complex-valued SAR measurements or native acquisition geometry, which restricts physically grounded multimodal learning. In particular, large-scale public datasets combining very-high-resolution (VHR) S
items.summary: Multimodal foundation models have advanced rapidly thanks to large optical benchmarks, but comparable resources for synthetic aperture radar (SAR) remain limited. Existing SAR--optical datasets largely rely on low-resolution, intensity-only Ground Range Detected~(GRD) products and do not preserve complex-valued SAR measurements or native acquisition geometry, which restricts physically grounded multimodal learning. In particular, large-scale public datasets combining very-high-resolution (VHR) SAR SLC, aligned optical imagery, and natural-language descriptions are still lacking. We present a VHR SAR--optical--text dataset built from open-access Umbra spotlight acquisitions distributed as Sensor Independent Complex Data (SICD). From around 2,500 worldwide scenes (VV/HH, 20cm--2m native resolution), we standardize all SAR data to an 80cm slant-range grid via band-limited FFT resampling and tile the imagery into 1024 by 1024 patches. For each SAR patch, we retrieve a high-resolution optical tile and warp it into the SAR grid using local coordinate correspondences for local pixel-level alignment. We further generate three caption variants (SHORT/MID/LONG) per sample to support vision--language training and evaluation. Our dataset contains 119,566 triplets (complex and amplitude slant-range SAR patch, aligned optical patch, natural-language description) covering 257 locations across 72 countries and a broad range of land types and infrastructures. We release fixed train/validation/test splits and the full preprocessing and baseline code to enable reproducible benchmarks for multimodal alignment on cross-modal retrieval and conditional generation in native SAR geometry. The dataset is publicly available on the Hugging Face Hub at https://huggingface.co/datasets/ONERA/SARLO-80.
items.author: Solène Debuysère, Nicolas Trouvé, Nathan Letheule, Elise Colin, Georgia Channing
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: alignment, benchmark, evaluation

We present a VHR SAR--optical--text dataset built from open-access Umbra spotlight acquisitions distributed as Sensor Independent Complex Data (SICD). From around 2,500 worldwide scenes (VV/HH, 20cm--2m native resolution), we standardize all SAR data to an 80cm slant-range grid via band-limited FFT resampling and tile the imagery into 1024 by 1024 patches. For each SAR patch, we retrieve a high-resolution optical tile and warp it into the SAR grid using local coordinate correspondences for local pixel-level alignment. We further generate three caption variants (SHORT/MID/LONG) per sample to support vision--language training and evaluation. Our dataset contains 119,566 triplets (complex and amplitude slant-range SAR patch, aligned optical patch, natural-language description) covering 257 locations across 72 countries and a broad range of land types and infrastructures. We release fixed train/validation/test splits and the full preprocessing and baseline code to enable reproducible benchmarks for multimodal alignment on cross-modal retrieval and conditional generation in native SAR geometry. The dataset is publicly available on the Hugging Face Hub at https://huggingface.co/datasets/ONERA/SARLO-80.

trendEntities.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
normalizedKeys: title:sovereign execution brokers enforcing certificate bound authority in agentic control planes

items.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
items.description: Autonomous agents are increasingly connected to cloud, deployment, and data-control workflows, but production mutation authority should not reside inside non-deterministic reasoning processes. Existing access-control mechanisms authorize identities, while assurance layers certify proposed actions; neither alone provides a mandatory enforcement point for certified authority at the moment of mutation. This paper introduces the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB), a runtime enforcement boundary for ce
items.summary: Autonomous agents are increasingly connected to cloud, deployment, and data-control workflows, but production mutation authority should not reside inside non-deterministic reasoning processes. Existing access-control mechanisms authorize identities, while assurance layers certify proposed actions; neither alone provides a mandatory enforcement point for certified authority at the moment of mutation. This paper introduces the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB), a runtime enforcement boundary for certificate-bound agentic infrastructure. SEB consumes certificates issued by the Sovereign Assurance Boundary (SAB), verifies that the requested mutation matches the certified execution contract, checks validity windows, policy epochs, revocation epochs, and live-state drift, mints scoped execution identity, invokes infrastructure APIs, and records signed decision and outcome records. By separating proposal, admission, and execution, SEB turns certified authority into a short-lived, revocable, auditable runtime capability, provided that production mutation APIs reject non-broker identities. We present the SEB execution model, certificate and replay-verification predicates, scoped identity semantics, bypass-prevention deployment patterns, failure behavior, and a concrete prototype implementation. We evaluate the prototype on AWS and Kubernetes clusters, measuring latency overheads, revocation propagation, drift detection, and security under fault injection.
items.author: Jun He, Deying Yu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, agentic, workflow

trendEntities.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
normalizedKeys: title:flowedit associative memory for lifelong pronunciation adaptation in flow matching tts

items.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
items.description: Flow-matching text-to-speech systems achieve remarkable zero-shot quality but remain static after deployment: pronunciation errors on out-of-vocabulary proper nouns persist unless the model is retrained. We introduce FlowEdit, a life-long adaptation framework for frozen flow-matching TTS that learns pronunciation corrections as latent conditioning edits rather than weight updates. When corrective feedback is provided, FlowEdit optimizes a token-level perturbation in the text embedding space, the
items.summary: Flow-matching text-to-speech systems achieve remarkable zero-shot quality but remain static after deployment: pronunciation errors on out-of-vocabulary proper nouns persist unless the model is retrained. We introduce FlowEdit, a life-long adaptation framework for frozen flow-matching TTS that learns pronunciation corrections as latent conditioning edits rather than weight updates. When corrective feedback is provided, FlowEdit optimizes a token-level perturbation in the text embedding space, then stores the correction in a Modern Hopfield Network serving as content-addressable episodic memory. At inference, corrections are retrieved via soft attention with a similarity gate, enabling fuzzy morphological matching. On our curated benchmark of 312 multilingual proper nouns across 18 language families, FlowEdit reduces target-word Phoneme Error Rate by 92.7% relative to the zero-shot baseline while maintaining identical general-speech quality. Corrections complete in approximately 15 seconds on a single GPU.
items.author: Harshit Singh, Ayush Pratap Singh, Nityanand Mathur
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: embedding, benchmark

trendEntities.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
normalizedKeys: title:multi lcb extending livecodebench to multiple programming languages

items.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
items.description: LiveCodeBench (LCB) has recently become a widely adopted benchmark for evaluating large language models (LLMs) on code-generation tasks. By curating competitive programming problems, constantly adding fresh problems to the set, and filtering them by release dates, LCB provides contamination-aware evaluation and offers a holistic view of coding capability. However, LCB remains restricted to Python, leaving open the question of whether LLMs can generalize across the diverse programming languages r
items.summary: LiveCodeBench (LCB) has recently become a widely adopted benchmark for evaluating large language models (LLMs) on code-generation tasks. By curating competitive programming problems, constantly adding fresh problems to the set, and filtering them by release dates, LCB provides contamination-aware evaluation and offers a holistic view of coding capability. However, LCB remains restricted to Python, leaving open the question of whether LLMs can generalize across the diverse programming languages required in real-world software engineering. We introduce Multi-LCB, a benchmark for evaluating LLMs across twelve programming languages, including Python. Multi-LCB transforms Python tasks from the LCB dataset into equivalent tasks in other languages while preserving LCB's contamination controls and evaluation protocol. Because it is fully compatible with the original LCB format, Multi-LCB will automatically track future LCB updates, enabling systematic assessment of cross-language code generation competence and requiring models to sustain performance well beyond Python. We evaluated 24 LLMs for instruction and reasoning on Multi-LCB, uncovering evidence of Python overfitting, language-specific contamination, and substantial disparities in multilingual performance. Our results establish Multi-LCB as a rigorous new benchmark for multi-programming-language code evaluation, directly addressing LCB's primary limitation and exposing critical gaps in current LLM capabilities.
items.author: Maria Ivanova, Pavel Zadorozhny, Rodion Levichev, Ivan Petrov, Adamenko Pavel, Ivan Lopatin, Alexey Kutalev, Dmitrii Babaev
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, code generation, software engineering, benchmark
